skill.name: azure-monitor-opentelemetry-py
skill.description: Guides Python teams through Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry setup for Application Insights, including auto-instrumentation, sampling, custom telemetry, and Azure AD authentication.
skill.summary: Set up Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry for Python applications.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, hardcoded URL, and weak cryptography patterns. Review found the command and weak cryptography hits are markdown or table false positives, while the URLs are expected Azure Application Insights examples. No prompt injection, malicious intent, data exfiltration logic, or executable skill code was found.

low_findings.title: Documented Package Installation Command
low_findings.description: The skill includes a pip install command for the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry package. This is expected installation guidance, but users should review package provenance before running dependency installation commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is visible in an installation section and installs the named package. It is not hidden or dynamically constructed, but it still asks users to run an external package manager.

low_findings.title: Expected Azure Telemetry Endpoint Examples
low_findings.description: The skill shows placeholder Application Insights connection strings with Azure ingestion endpoints. This is consistent with Azure Monitor setup and does not show unauthorized exfiltration, but telemetry destinations should be verified by users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are Azure Application Insights ingestion endpoint examples tied to the stated purpose of the skill. The values are placeholders and no secret or unrelated destination is present.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Matches Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Most static command execution findings point to markdown code fences or inline documentation around Python examples. No executable Ruby backtick usage or skill-side shell execution was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are markdown code fences, Python snippets, or a parameter table. The skill contains only SKILL.md and no executable wrapper that would run these snippets automatically.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts point to the YAML description area and a configuration table header. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing implementation, or weak cipher usage appears at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are metadata text and a markdown table header. There is no code or cryptographic primitive to evaluate at those locations.

content.user_title: Configure Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry for Python
content.value_statement: Python teams need consistent observability without custom tracing setup for every service. This skill explains Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry configuration for Application Insights, auto-instrumentation, sampling, logs, metrics, and Azure authentication.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Python package.
Provides setup examples for Flask, Django, and FastAPI applications.
Covers custom traces, custom metrics, and application logs.
Documents sampling, cloud role names, selected instrumentations, live metrics, and Azure AD authentication.
limitations: It is a documentation skill, not an automated deployment tool.
It does not validate Azure resources or connection strings.
It does not cover every OpenTelemetry exporter or collector topology.
It assumes the project can install the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry package.

use_cases.title: Add Observability to a Python Web Service
use_cases.description: Use the skill to add Application Insights telemetry to a Flask, Django, or FastAPI service with minimal setup.

use_cases.title: Standardize Azure Monitoring Guidance
use_cases.description: Use the skill to align service teams on connection strings, service names, sampling, and authentication choices.

use_cases.title: Review Telemetry Configuration Options
use_cases.description: Use the skill to compare custom traces, metrics, logs, live metrics, and selected instrumentations before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Start Basic Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the basic steps to configure Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry in a Python application.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new service

prompt_templates.title: Adapt for a Web Framework
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry for my FastAPI application and explain where setup should run.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Production Telemetry
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my planned Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry settings for sampling, cloud role name, logs, and live metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Design Secure Azure Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry plan that uses Azure AD authentication and avoids storing connection strings in code.

output_examples.input: Configure Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry for a new Python API.
output_examples.output: A concise setup plan covering package installation, environment configuration, early initialization, and verification in Application Insights.

output_examples.input: Explain how to reduce telemetry volume for a high-traffic service.
output_examples.output: A production-focused recommendation that uses sampling, service naming, structured logs, and custom attributes for useful diagnostics.

output_examples.input: Help me avoid hardcoded secrets in observability setup.
output_examples.output: Guidance to use environment variables or Azure AD authentication, with reminders to keep connection strings out of source control.

best_practices: Configure Azure Monitor before importing instrumented libraries.
Store connection strings in environment variables or use Azure AD authentication.
Set service names and sampling policies for production workloads.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode real Application Insights connection strings in source files.
Do not enable full telemetry volume for high-traffic services without planning cost and retention.
Do not add custom attributes that contain secrets or personal data.

faq.question: Does this skill run code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and examples for configuring Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry in Python projects.

faq.question: Which Python frameworks are covered?
faq.answer: The skill includes examples for Flask, Django, and FastAPI, plus general Python telemetry patterns.

faq.question: Does it require an Application Insights connection string?
faq.answer: The examples show connection string usage, but the skill also mentions Azure AD authentication with DefaultAzureCredential.

faq.question: Can it configure custom traces and metrics?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows how to create spans, add attributes, create counters, and send logs to Application Insights.

faq.question: Is the Azure endpoint in the examples suspicious?
faq.answer: No. The endpoint is a placeholder Azure Application Insights ingestion URL and matches the skill purpose.

faq.question: Is this a complete OpenTelemetry operations guide?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Distro basics and selected production configuration options.
